name: Zendesk
one_liner: Customer support and helpdesk platform for managing tickets, knowledge base, and omnichannel customer conversations.
primary_auth: API token (email/token via Basic auth) for server-side scripts; OAuth2 for distributed apps
access_notes: Zendesk offers a free trial (typically 14 days) that provisions a working subdomain and full API access. API tokens and OAuth clients can be created self-serve from the admin center; no human approval needed. After trial, a paid plan is required to keep the instance. Note: API tokens are being deprecated (deactivated April 30, 2027) in favor of OAuth.
api_notes: Extensive REST API covering Ticketing (tickets, users, organizations, comments, macros, triggers, automations, views), Help Center/Guide, Talk, Chat, Sell, and admin resources. Well over 100 endpoints. Cursor-based pagination, incremental exports, and sideloading supported.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server exposing a Zendesk instance to agents. Multiple community/third-party servers exist (reminia/zendesk-mcp-server on GitHub, Swifteq, Zapier, CData). Zendesk did announce an 'MCP Client' for its own Copilot/AI Agents to consume external MCP servers, but that is the inverse direction and not a server exposing Zendesk data.
main_blocker: None significant; API access is self-serve via trial or paid account. Only mild friction is the upcoming API-token deprecation pushing toward OAuth.

evidence.claim: Zendesk supports API token (Basic auth email/token) and OAuth Bearer token authentication; must be a verified user.
OAuth access tokens used as Bearer tokens; authorization code and client credentials flows documented.
API tokens will be deactivated on April 30, 2027, with migration to OAuth recommended.
No official vendor MCP server; community and third-party MCP servers are the available options.

open_questions: Exact current trial length and whether all API resources (e.g., Talk, Sell) are fully enabled during trial vs. gated by plan tier.
